steps.title: Determine Required Resistance
steps.description: Calculate the resistance value needed using Ohm's Law. Consider the operating voltage and desired current.

steps.title: Calculate Power Dissipation
steps.description: Use P = I²R or P = V²/R to determine power. Select a resistor with at least 2× the calculated power rating.

steps.title: Select Tolerance
steps.description: Standard circuits: ±5% (carbon film). Precision circuits: ±1% or better (metal film).

steps.title: Consider Temperature Requirements
steps.description: For precision or high-temperature environments, select resistors with low temperature coefficients (<±100 ppm/°C).

steps.title: Choose Package Type
steps.description: Through-hole for prototyping and high power. SMD for production and space-constrained designs.

tips: Always derate power to 50% or less for reliability
Use metal film resistors for audio and precision circuits
For high-voltage circuits, verify the maximum voltage rating
Consider using multiple resistors in series/parallel for high-voltage or high-power applications
